items.title: Putin-Macron talks and the Russia expertise gap
items.digest: In this Feb. 14, 2022 Moscow Times opinion piece, Ilya Klishin argues that a small factual error in foreign reporting around Putin-Macron talks exposed a larger expertise problem in commentary about Russia. The trigger is a repeated claim that Putin quoted a specific punk song after his meeting with Emmanuel Macron.
items.quote: "every word spoken carries weight" - The Moscow Times
items.summary: 14, 2022 Moscow Times opinion piece, Ilya Klishin argues that a small factual error in foreign reporting around Putin-Macron talks exposed a larger expertise problem in commentary about Russia.

items.title: Mass protests: lessons from a decade
items.digest: In this Dec. 10, 2021 Moscow Times essay, Klishin revisits the 2011-2012 protest wave and questions the binary verdict that the movement simply failed. He traces how mass street mobilization shifted political language, expectations, and civic habits even without immediate institutional change. The piece argues that outcomes in authoritarian settings often unfold on delayed timelines: visible defeats can still alter social coordination, public memory, and future cycles of resistance. A decade later, the text frames protest history as a long process, not a one-season event.
items.quote: "those demonstrations ended in defeat" - The Moscow Times

items.title: Mandatory vaccination and public resistance
items.digest: In this June 18, 2021 Moscow Times commentary, Klishin examines why Russia's vaccination rollout stalled even though domestic vaccines were available, free, and widely distributed. The article contrasts two communication realities: high state capacity for political messaging and low persuasive impact in public-health trust building. It describes a social split in vaccine uptake, noting stronger willingness among younger urban groups and deeper skepticism among audiences usually aligned with state narratives.
items.quote: "People simply don’t want to get vaccinated!" - The Moscow Times

items.digest: La carte reconstitue le dossier avec dates, acteurs nommes et contexte editorial afin de distinguer faits rapportes et interpretation. Au lieu de s appuyer sur des etiquettes generales, le texte reconstruit le dossier avec acteurs nommes, sequence temporelle et cadre institutionnel de publication.

items.title: Kontext statt Etikett
items.digest: Der Eintrag ordnet das Thema ueber datierte Ereignisse, benannte Akteure und redaktionellen Kontext ein, damit berichtete Fakten von Interpretation getrennt bleiben. Statt kategorischer Etiketten setzt der Text auf Verhaeltnis, Praezision und Nachpruefbarkeit, besonders in Phasen hoher Unsicherheit. Statt mit pauschalen Etiketten zu arbeiten, rekonstruiert der Text den Fall ueber benannte Akteure, zeitliche Abfolge und institutionellen Publikationskontext.

items.title: Holod interview with Ilya Klishin
items.digest: This July 13, 2023 interview in Holod gives an operational view of how the Volna media project was launched and scaled for Russian-speaking relocants in Lithuania, Latvia, Germany, and other destinations. Klishin describes the product logic as service-first: daily local digests, practical orientation, and lower-anxiety information flow for people adapting to new legal and social environments. The conversation explains why Volna did not copy high-frequency conflict feeds from major exile outlets; instead, it prioritized usable local context, from transport and documents to civic events, while keeping an explicit anti-war position.
items.quote: "Respect for the host country" - Holod

items.title: Likes Go West
items.digest:
In a remarkably prescient May 2014 column for Vedomosti-published more than two full years before the 2016 US presidential election and the ensuing global scandal over Russian cyber interference-Klishin exposed the Kremlin’s nascent digital operations targeting Western democracies. Relying on exclusive sources close to the Presidential Administration, leaked files from "Anonymous International," and insights from European media executives, the piece detailed the strategic pivot of domestic troll farms toward American and European audiences.

Klishin dismantled the emerging operational mindset of these state-sponsored networks. He documented their early structural and financial foundations, revealing how they tested synthetic engagement, studied the pre-moderation algorithms of major outlets like The Huffington Post and Politico, and refined their use of provocative, polarized messaging. This investigation not only mapped the exact infrastructure of the new "foreign departments" but accurately predicted the weaponization of social media long before coordinated online manipulation became a defining issue of global politics.
items.quote: "Work aimed at the West was only beginning" - Vedomosti

items.title: TV Rain launched "Our Soldiers" civilian investigation
items.digest: This August 2014 TV Rain project page introduces "Our Soldiers," a public-facing investigation into reports of Russian servicemen killed, missing, or captured near the Ukrainian border during a period of official denials. The framing is explicit: the goal is not rhetorical escalation but factual clarification of identities, numbers, and circumstances. The page states that if authorities withhold information, independent verification must be built from open submissions, witness reports, and case-level documentation.

items.title: L avenir annule: politique du present en temps de crise
items.digest: Dans cette chronique publiee par Vedomosti le 20 mai 2020, Ilia Klishin analyse le changement de temporalite politique provoque par la crise sanitaire. Son point central est que la memoire du passe recent s affaiblit tandis que l avenir devient moins lisible, ce qui deplace l agenda public vers une gestion du present immediat.

items.title: "Body here, soul there." How 19th century Russian emigre press worked
